test_suite.details: 99 failures are all in predictions schema (PRED/DW/MT entries with 'logging'/'unresolved' outcomes and test_window objects). These are known schema evolution items, not structural regressions. Core WIN/HTML/link tests all pass.

internal_anchors.details: All 97 unique internal href targets resolve to existing elements in docs/index.html. Test suite Section 3 independently confirms this.

tab_structure.details: 11 tabs (overview through refs), all with unique IDs and headings. Nav chain is continuous: overview→evaluate→model→selftest→wins→pages→killshots→predictions→falsify→ai→refs. Active tab on load is 'overview'. No orphaned tabs.

heading_hierarchy.details: All 10 content tabs start with h1. No skipped heading levels detected.

timeouts.note: NASA JPL PODAAC - known slow government server

external_links.details: 55 of 56 non-DOI external links OK. 1 timeout (JPL PODAAC PDF - government site, likely transient). No broken links.

data_prose_consistency.details: Build produces total=69 (base 3-digit WINs, excluding WIN-058b sub-ID). wins.json has 70 entries; the 70th (058b) is a dome numbering collision tracker, correctly excluded from the headline count. Verdict tallies match: Refuted=9, Self-Contradicted=13, Misleading=23, Std Model=17, Not Demonstrated=4, Unfalsifiable=4. new_in_v51=31. All counts computed from data at build time.

win_detail_consistency.details: 70 WIN anchor IDs (win001–win069 plus win058b) present in HTML. 76 summary table rows (includes header/group rows). 8 detail groups with consistent cross-references. All verdict tags present for each category.

win_number_alignment.details: Dome site fetch not attempted this run (network rate limiting). Poller handles ongoing alignment tracking.

discoverability.llms_txt: exists, mentions review URL and dome model
discoverability.sitemap_xml: exists, valid urlset, contains main URL
discoverability.robots_txt: exists, has Allow:/ and Sitemap directive
discoverability.uncounted_failures: 8 entries, no duplicate IDs, all FAIL-NNN format, all required fields present
discoverability.meta_tags: description, og:title, og:description, ClaimReview LD+JSON all present
discoverability.details: All discoverability infrastructure intact and well-formed.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id=60 > max_id=59: OK. One ID gap: EXP-052 (possible write collision or manual deletion). 20 orphaned expansion files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ that have no tracker entry — these are likely legacy files from before the tracker was introduced (e.g., EXP-004 through EXP-014 with old naming convention). No phantom entries or stale pending items.

priority_queue_coupling.details: MAJOR: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup, status=complete) has no matching entry in priority-queue.json. This means the prose writeup will bypass curmudgeon adversarial review.

workspace_sync_skips.details: MAJOR: 4 files with repeated direction-violation skips in the last 24h. morning-briefing.txt is classified workspace-owned so should not be skipped — this may indicate a workspace-sync bug or misclassification. CAT-005.json and PROP-003/004.json are in append-only directories — the workspace-sync agent may be trying to overwrite existing files rather than creating new ones, triggering the direction guard incorrectly.

workspace_only_files.details: 187 curmudgeon reviews and 71 analyst expansions on workspace. Cannot compare against git from FUSE mount — workspace-sync agent handles the git push. Cross-check with git clone would need a separate run.

claude_md_checks.details: No hardcoded counts found in CLAUDE.md. 9 of 10 checked file paths exist. One bare reference 'wins.json' (without data/ prefix) in an inline code command — not a real issue since the command provides context.

build_reproducibility.details: node build.js html produces identical output to current docs/index.html. No build drift.

issues_found.description: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup, status=complete) is not represented in the curmudgeon priority queue. The prose writeup will either never receive adversarial review or will bypass the churn-and-burn pipeline.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should push EXP-050 onto priority-queue.json with target_id='EXP-050' and appropriate context_hints.

issues_found.description: 4 files with repeated workspace-sync direction-violation skips in the last 24h: CAT-005.json (5), morning-briefing.txt (4), PROP-003.json (4), PROP-004.json (4). This indicates writers are editing git-owned files from the workspace side, or the direction guard is misclassifying append-only files.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate which agent is writing to these paths from the workspace. morning-briefing.txt is workspace-owned and should NOT be skipped — check if workspace-sync.md has it in the wrong ownership list. For append-only dirs, verify the direction guard allows new file creation but blocks overwrites.

issues_found.description: 20 orphaned expansion output files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ have no matching tracker entry. These are completed work that the decider pipeline cannot discover.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Most appear to be legacy files (EXP-004 through EXP-014 with old naming convention, plus EXP-019–027). Decider should reconcile by either adding tracker entries or archiving orphans. EXP-060.json and ISS-*.json are newer and need immediate attention.

issues_found.description: Expansion tracker has an ID gap at EXP-052. This may indicate a write collision or manual deletion.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate whether EXP-052 was created and lost, or if the ID was skipped during allocation. Check git history for the tracker file.

issues_found.description: NASA JPL PODAAC URL timed out: https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/ProdSpecDoc_v4.6.pdf
issues_found.suggested_fix: Government data sites are often slow. Verify on next run — if persistent, check if PODAAC has migrated this document to a new URL.

summary: Build is reproducible (0 diff lines), all internal anchors resolve, tab structure and nav chain are intact, and discoverability infrastructure is complete. Three major issues found: (1) EXP-050 category-proposal-writeup is missing from the curmudgeon priority queue, bypassing adversarial review; (2) repeated workspace-sync direction-violation skips on 4 files suggest a writer or classification bug; (3) 20 orphaned expansion output files lack tracker entries. One moderate ID gap at EXP-052 and one external link timeout (NASA JPL PODAAC). Test suite shows 2751 passed / 99 failed — all failures are in predictions schema evolution, not structural regressions.
